MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Nov-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Michael Hoffman, appeared for Plaintiff/moving party
George Leal, appeared for Defendant/opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Sets Of Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is denied as to No. 17. The amended response is adequate.

The motion is denied as to Nos. 20 and 23. The motion is granted as to Nos. 26, 29, 32, 35, and 38.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 22, 25, 28, 31, 34, 37, 40, and 43 as they are irrelevant and invades privacy of other employees without good cause.
The motion is denied as to No. 103 as it is irrelevant.
The responses shall be subject to a protective order.
Further responses shall be made no later than November 18, 2005.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
Off Calendar Nov-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Michael Hoffman, appeared for Plaintiff/moving party
George Leal, appeared for Defendant/opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Sets Of Request For Inspection of Documents and Production of Documents is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to No. 22, limited to documents which set forth gross sales for the wholesale division for each fiscal year beginning July 1997-June 2003.
The documents shall be made available for inspection at a time to be agreed upon by Defendant's counsel and Plaintiff for a date no later than November 18, 2005. The documents made available for inspection shall be subject to a protective order limiting use of the documents to this litigation.
All requests for sanctions are denied. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
